techniques.evidence_quote: Using an access token that had been compromised.
Using an access token that had been compromised.
The actor compromised a service account... this account had privileges to take a variety of actions within Sign's production environment.
a third party gained access to a Dropbox Sign automated system configuration tool
used this access to the production environment to access our customer database

attack_chain.description: Threat actor obtained a compromised access token for Dropbox Sign infrastructure (exact method of theft not disclosed).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Using an access token that had been compromised.

attack_chain.description: Using the compromised access token, the attacker gained initial access to a Dropbox Sign automated system configuration tool on April 19, 2024.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a third party gained access to a Dropbox Sign automated system configuration tool

evidence_chunks.quote: a third party gained access to a Dropbox Sign automated system configuration tool
a third party gained access to a Dropbox Sign automated system configuration tool

attack_chain.description: Attacker compromised a service account (non-human back-end account) used to run automated services, inheriting its elevated privileges in the production environment.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The actor compromised a service account that was part of Sign's back-end... this account had privileges to take a variety of actions within Sign's production environment.

attack_chain.description: Using the service account's privileges, the attacker accessed the Dropbox Sign customer database containing emails, names, phone numbers, hashed passwords, API keys, OAuth tokens, and MFA data.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The threat actor then used this access to the production environment to access our customer database.

evidence_chunks.quote: The threat actor then used this access to the production environment to access our customer database.
The threat actor then used this access to the production environment to access our customer database.

attack_chain.description: Attacker exfiltrated customer authentication data (emails, names, hashed passwords, API keys, OAuth tokens, MFA data). Last observed activity April 20, 2024.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a threat actor had accessed data including Dropbox Sign customer information such as email addresses, user names, phone numbers and hashed passwords... API keys, OAuth tokens, and multi-factor authentication

initial_access_vector: Compromised access token used to gain entry into a Dropbox Sign automated system configuration tool.
persistence_mechanism: Use of compromised service account credentials (no malware observed).
privilege_escalation: Compromise of a highly privileged non-human service account used for automated back-end services in Sign's production environment.
